title: Ophiomeris espirito-santensis
taxonomy: Ophiomeris espirito-santensis is classified within the family Berberidaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the barberry family. This family consists of about 630 species and is native to the Northern Hemisphere. Ophiomeris espirito-santensis is further classified within the genus Ophiomeris. The genus Ophiomeris is part of the order Ranunculales, which includes a variety of flowering plants. The classification of Ophiomeris espirito-santensis is still a subject of ongoing research.

intro: Ophiomeris espirito-santensis, also known as "Ophiomeris," is a species of flowering plant in the family Berberidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with various conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ophiomeris espirito-santensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ophiomeris is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues.
